query: Death Valley Furnace Creek DEVC1 station highest daily maximum temperature recorded so far July 2026, actual observations July 7-13 2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary:

## Death Valley Furnace Creek DEVC1 — Highest Daily Max So Far, July 2026

**The highest daily maximum temperature recorded so far in July 2026 at the DEVC1 station is 48.17°C ≈ 118.7°F (rounds to 119°F), observed on July 11, 2026.**

### Actual observed daily maxima (July 7–13, 2026)
Derived from NWS DEVC1 hourly observations (UTC timestamps converted to local PDT, since Death Valley's afternoon peak appears in the feed around 23:00–02:00 UTC):

| Local Date | Daily Max |
|---|---|
| July 7 | 46.5°C / 115.7°F |
| July 8 | 47.0°C / 116.6°F |
| July 9 | 47.78°C / **118.0°F** |
| July 10 | 47.5°C / 117.5°F |
| **July 11** | **48.17°C / 118.7°F** ← highest |
| July 12 | 45.06°C / 113.1°F |
| July 13 | Partial (afternoon peak not yet reached at research time) |

### Key context
- **Cross-validated** by AccuWeather daily data (highs of 47–48°C Jul 7–11, 46°C Jul 12–13) and Weather Underground.
- These values are **notably moderate** for Death Valley in July — below typical July extremes (120°F+) and far below records (134°F on July 10, 1913; 130°F in 2021 and 2023).
- A **cooling dip** occurred July 12, with a renewed warm-up toward ~119–120°F forecast around July 15–16.

One important methodological note for the forecaster: I mapped hourly UTC readings back to **local calendar days**. If a resolution source instead uses a UTC-day or a different daily-max definition, the July 11 peak (logged 01:00 UTC July 12) could be attributed slightly differently — but by local time, **July 11 is the hottest day so far at 118.7°F**.
